title: Kelleria lyallii
intro: Kelleria lyallii, also known as New Zealand rock Daisy or Kowhai, is a small, shrub-like plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in New Zealand for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms associated with colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Kelleria lyallii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Kelleria lyallii is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support respiratory health and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Kelleria lyallii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This family is part of the order Oxalidales and the superorder Rosanae.
